article_meta.title: Once upon a time, school students would submit an essay, and teachers would mark it

reasoning: Good attribution discipline and mostly solid labels with defensible instances. However, it missed several clear author-level bias cues (loaded verbs/phrases and an unattributed claim), so recall is lacking.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged items are author framing/structure or captions; no quoted source language was tagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Flagged items (slant emphasis, subjective adjectives, elite-source emphasis) are reasonable and supported by the article’s structure and wording.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed notable author-prose bias: loaded phrasing like 'interrogate them' and 'put them on the spot'; rhetorical spin ('Enter “Thinking Mode”'); and an unattributed claim ('Early results show it has saved time...').
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels fit the instances: Slant for structural emphasis, Subjective Qualifying Adjectives for 'innovative'/'brand-new', and Elite vs. Populist for source selection lean toward institutions.
